title: External-Local Scope Media Layout
summary: Keep ordinary external-local published media beneath its external published-documents root and under the same scope lifecycle as repo-backed local media.
content_html:
External-Local Scope Media Layout

Outcome

External-local scopes use the same source/published separation as repo-backed local scopes:

source/<scope>/
  documents/*.md
  media/mermaid/*.mmd       # when configured

published/docs/<scope>/
  by-id/*.json
  media/
    img/
    svg/
    files/

For external published media this is:

$DOTLINEFORM_PROJECTS_BASE_DIR/docs-viewer/published/docs/<scope>/media/

The browser contract remains /docs/media/<scope>/<type>/<filename> for locally served configured media types.

For every configured local scope, the registered local img and files directories are materialized even when they contain no media. They are part of the visible workspace contract, not virtual paths that appear only on first write.

Ownership

External describes the storage location, not a different ownership model. Ordinary imported media and authored figures are managed by Docs Viewer in both local scope types:

  • create establishes the separate scope source and published roots plus registered local media directories;
  • local service startup restores any missing configured media directories;
  • rename moves published media with the published-documents root;
  • delete removes the manifest-owned source and published roots, including media.

Config loading remains read-only. Materialization belongs to local-service startup and confirmed scope creation, where a failure to establish the configured directories is visible rather than deferred until an import. Repo-backed empty directories use tracked marker files so the structure also survives a clean clone.

Generated docs, search data, and published media remain separate from canonical source in configured published/ roots.

The former docs-viewer/source/<scope>/media/<published-type>/ paths are not compatibility lookups. Existing published bytes must be moved manually before adopting this boundary; canonical build-source directories such as media/mermaid/ remain beneath source.

Separate Asset-Collection Boundary

External Asset Collections inventory user-owned files that are not ordinary scope media. Their roots stay outside the registered scope source and published roots, are not named or derived by scope lifecycle, and are never moved or deleted merely because the associated scope is renamed or deleted.

Code Owners

  • docs_scope_config.py validates each registered published media location and confines external-local media beneath the scope's published media root.
  • docs_media_storage.py, docs_import_media.py, and docs_import_markdown_package.py resolve and materialize imports through the common location adapter.
  • docs_scope_rename.py moves external source and published roots; media moves with the published-documents root.
  • scope deletion removes the manifest-owned source and published roots.

Source Organisation, Scope Lifecycle, and Media And Asset Handling own the durable current contracts.
